Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and low total CO₂, which may indicate mild metabolic acidosis. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), raising concern for possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and clinical correlation for underlying metabolic or renal pathology.
